html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 高坡異纂 - Title of the work, meaning 'High Slope Odd Collection' or 'High Slope Strange Tales.'
  • 京邸之寓名 - Refers to a specific name for a place in the capital, '京邸' means 'capital residence,' and '寓名' means 'name of the place.'
  • 素不信元怪之談 - Means 'had never believed in strange and supernatural stories' or 'had never trusted tales of the supernatural.'
  • 傾心 - Means 'to be deeply convinced' or 'to be fully persuaded.'
  • 齊東之語 - Refers to a story that is false or unreliable, often used to describe something that is not true or is a fabrication.
  • 公然敢於侮天 - Means 'openly dared to insult heaven,' suggesting a bold or disrespectful act towards the divine.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 楊儀 - A Ming dynasty scholar and official, known for his works such as *Chitou Mìyǔ* (Chimney Mouth Secrets). He is the author of *Gāopō Yìzuǎn*.
  • 王維賢 - A historical figure mentioned in the text, though specific details about him are not provided in the source text.
  • 劉基 - A famous Ming dynasty statesman and military strategist, known for his contributions to the founding of the Ming dynasty.
  • 黃澤 - A renowned scholar from the late Yuan and early Ming dynasties, known for his expertise in Confucian classics.
  • 趙汸 - A scholar who studied under Huang Ze and was known for his commentaries on the *Book of Changes*.
  • 織女 - The Weaver Girl, a figure from Chinese mythology, often associated with the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl.
  • 文曲星 - The Star of Literature, a celestial being associated with literary talent and success.
  • 牽牛 - The Cowherd, another figure from Chinese mythology, often paired with the Weaver Girl in romantic stories.

Proper Nouns

  • 楊儀 - The author of *Gāopō Yìzuǎn*.
  • 王維賢 - A historical figure mentioned in the text.
  • 劉基 - A famous Ming dynasty statesman and military strategist.
  • 黃澤 - A renowned scholar from the late Yuan and early Ming dynasties.
  • 趙汸 - A scholar who studied under Huang Ze.
  • 織女 - The Weaver Girl, a figure from Chinese mythology.
  • 文曲星 - The Star of Literature, a celestial being.
  • 牽牛 - The Cowherd, another figure from Chinese mythology.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 出《四库总目提要》 - Indicates the text is excerpted from *The General Catalogue of the Four Treasuries*.
  • 螭头密语 - A book title, likely referring to a collection of intimate or secret conversations.
  • 旧本题明杨仪撰 - The old edition attributes the work to Yang Yi of the Ming dynasty.
  • 语多不经 - Many of the statements are unreliable or unverified.
  • 建文帝从隧道出亡 - The Jianwen Emperor escaped through a tunnel.
  • 仁宗中毒 - The Renzong Emperor was poisoned.
  • 宣宗微行 - The Xuangong Emperor went incognito.
  • 里巷无稽之谈 - Rumors from the streets that are baseless.
  • 所志孝宗武宗佚事 - The anecdotes about the Xiaozong and Wuzong Emperors.
  • 尤涉鄙俚 - Especially vulgar and crude.
  • 《常熟志》载仪所著有《南官集》、《高坡异纂》 - According to the *Changshu Gazetteer*, Yi wrote *Nanguan Collection* and *Gaopo Strange Compilation*.
  • 疑或出於伪托也 - It is suspected to be a forgery.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 四库总目提要 - *The General Catalogue of the Four Treasuries*, a massive bibliographic work compiled during the Qing dynasty.
  • 明杨仪 - Yang Yi, a Ming dynasty official and writer.
  • 嘉靖丙戌 - The year Bingshu of the Jiajing era (1546).
  • 山东按察司副使 - A Ming dynasty official position, equivalent to a deputy censor of Shandong.
  • 建文帝 - The Jianwen Emperor, who ruled briefly before being deposed by his uncle, the Yongle Emperor.
  • 仁宗 - The Renzong Emperor, the third emperor of the Ming dynasty.
  • 宣宗 - The Xuangong Emperor, the fifth emperor of the Ming dynasty.
  • 孝宗 - The Xiaozong Emperor, the eighth emperor of the Ming dynasty.
  • 武宗 - The Wuzong Emperor, the ninth emperor of the Ming dynasty.
  • 常熟志 - The *Changshu Gazetteer*, a local historical record.
  • 南官集 - *Nanguan Collection*, a work attributed to Yang Yi.
  • 高坡异纂 - *Gaopo Strange Compilation*, another work attributed to Yang Yi.

Proper Nouns

  • 四库总目提要 - *The General Catalogue of the Four Treasuries*.
  • 螭头密语 - A book title.
  • 明杨仪 - Yang Yi, a Ming dynasty official and writer.
  • 嘉靖丙戌 - The year Bingshu of the Jiajing era (1546).
  • 山东按察司副使 - A Ming dynasty official position.
  • 建文帝 - The Jianwen Emperor.
  • 仁宗 - The Renzong Emperor.
  • 宣宗 - The Xuangong Emperor.
  • 孝宗 - The Xiaozong Emperor.
  • 武宗 - The Wuzong Emperor.
  • 常熟志 - The *Changshu Gazetteer*.
  • 南官集 - *Nanguan Collection*.
  • 高坡异纂 - *Gaopo Strange Compilation*.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 心窃厌之 - Secretly detested or felt a hidden aversion.
  • 一见即弃去 - To discard immediately upon seeing, indicating a strong reaction.
  • 天地造化之妙 - The wonders of the universe's creation and transformation.
  • 有无相乘，终始相循 - The interplay of existence and non-existence, and the cyclical nature of beginnings and endings.
  • 梦想声色，倏忽变幻 - Fleeting and ephemeral experiences of desire and illusion.
  • 至理流行 - The operation of ultimate truth or the flow of fundamental principles.
  • 暂而不能久变而不能常 - Things that are temporary and cannot endure, or that change but do not remain constant.
  • 精思而详察之 - To think deeply and observe carefully.
  • 诞妄 - Falsehood or nonsense.
  • 文字交游 - Friends through literature or intellectual companions.
  • 凿凿皆有依据 - Solidly based on evidence or firmly grounded in fact.
  • 休沐 - A day off or rest day.
  • 去其鄙亵 - To remove the vulgar and indecent.
  • 琐屑谀谈 - Trivial and flattering talk.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 正德嘉靖间 - The reigns of Emperors Zhengde and Jiajing of the Ming dynasty, a period marked by political and cultural shifts.
  • 京师 - Refers to the capital city of Beijing during the Ming and Qing dynasties.
  • 仲秋六日 - The sixth day of the eighth lunar month, a specific date in the traditional Chinese calendar.

Proper Nouns

  • 高坡异纂 - The title of the collection being described.
  • 杨仪 - The author of the text, a scholar during the Ming dynasty.
  • 正德 - The reign title of Emperor Zhengde of the Ming dynasty.
  • 嘉靖 - The reign title of Emperor Jiajing of the Ming dynasty.
  • 京师 - The capital city of Beijing during the Ming and Qing dynasties.
  • 祥符高氏子业繁昌，谢氏凤仪 - Specific individuals or families mentioned in the text.
  • 仲秋六日 - The sixth day of the eighth lunar month.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 忽患心疾 - Suddenly suffered from a mental or emotional disorder.
  • 隐语嫚词 - Obscure and disrespectful words.
  • 入火不热，入水不溺 - Can enter fire without feeling heat and water without drowning.
  • 端坐凛然 - Sitting upright with a dignified and imposing manner.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 周颠仙 - A legendary figure known for his eccentric behavior and supernatural abilities.
  • 高皇帝 - Refers to the Hongwu Emperor (Zhu Yuanzhang), the founder of the Ming dynasty.
  • 南昌府 - The administrative region of Nanchang, a city in Jiangxi Province.
  • 建业 - The capital of the early Ming dynasty, now known as Nanjing.
  • 蒋山寺 - Jiangshan Temple, a Buddhist temple located in Nanchang.

Proper Nouns

  • 周颠仙 - Zhou Dianxian, a legendary figure.
  • 建昌 - Jianchang, a place name.
  • 高皇帝 - The Hongwu Emperor (Zhu Yuanzhang).
  • 南昌府 - Nanchang, an administrative region.
  • 建业 - Jianye, the capital of the early Ming dynasty (now Nanjing).
  • 蒋山寺 - Jiangshan Temple, a Buddhist temple in Nanchang.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 月余 - After a month.
  • 因不食半月矣 - Therefore, he has not eaten for half a month.
  • 帝亲往察之 - The emperor personally went to inspect him.
  • 颠仙广步来迎，殊无饥色 - The immortal walked broadly to greet him, showing no signs of hunger.
  • 帝具馔于翠微亭，召赐同燕 - The emperor prepared a feast at the Cuiwei Pavilion and invited him to share the banquet.
  • 将还，密诏主僧绝其饮食 - On his way back, he secretly ordered the head monk to stop his food and drink.
  • 积二十三日 - After accumulating twenty-three days.
  • 帝又往赐之食，则复食 - The emperor went again to offer him food, and he resumed eating.
  • 未几，将西征陈友谅 - Soon, the emperor was about to launch a western campaign against Chen Youliang.
  • 颠仙仰面上视，良久，正色摇手曰 - The immortal looked up, stared for a long time, and sternly shook his hand, saying...
  • 天命不在，友谅可征也 - Heaven's mandate is not with him; Chen Youliang can be conquered.
  • 已而举杖导帝马前，奋迅疾行，为壮士挥戈之势，以示必胜 - Later, he raised his staff to guide the emperor's horse forward, moving swiftly and energetically, demonstrating the posture of a valiant warrior wielding a spear, indicating certain victory.
  • 因令从征，师抵小孤山 - He was then ordered to join the campaign, and the army reached Xiaogushan.
  • 见江豚戏水中，忽出谬说，言水怪见损人多 - They saw a river dolphin playing in the water, and suddenly, he made a mistaken statement, saying that water spirits had caused many injuries.
  • 帝恶之，命将士引去，弃湖口水中，不能溺 - The emperor disliked it, ordered the soldiers to leave, and threw him into the water at the mouth of the lake, but he did not drown.
  • 明日复从诸卒至，求食于帝 - The next day, he followed the soldiers back, seeking food from the emperor.
  • 食既，整装而行，自是不复来见矣 - After eating, he packed up and left, never to be seen again.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 颠仙 - Refers to a Daoist immortal or a person with supernatural abilities.
  • 沙弥 - A novice monk in Buddhism.
  • 翠微亭 - A pavilion named Cuiwei, likely a place of significance in the court or palace.
  • 陈友谅 - A historical figure, a rebel leader during the late Yuan and early Ming dynasties.
  • 江豚 - A type of dolphin found in rivers, often associated with folklore and superstitions.

Proper Nouns

  • 颠仙 - The name of the immortal.
  • 沙弥 - The term for a novice monk.
  • 翠微亭 - The name of the pavilion.
  • 陈友谅 - The name of the historical figure.
  • 江豚 - The name of the river dolphin.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 更數年 - Means 'several years later,' indicating a passage of time.
  • 遣天池寺赤脚僧 - Means 'sent a barefoot monk from Tiantchi Temple,' referring to a messenger or envoy.
  • 帝以诗二首寄之 - Means 'the emperor sent two poems to him,' indicating a literary exchange.
  • 又四年帝偶不豫 - Means 'another four years later, the emperor happened to feel unwell,' where '不豫' is a euphemism for illness.
  • 言天眼尊者及颠仙从庐山竹林寺遣送药来 - Means 'said that Tiantian Zunzhe and Diandian Xian had sent medicine from Zhulin Temple on Mount Lu,' referring to divine or supernatural intervention.
  • 帝强起视药 - Means 'the emperor forced himself to look at the medicine,' indicating his determination to recover.
  • 温良药两片 - Means 'two pieces of Wenliang medicine,' where '温良' is a compound term for a specific type of medicine.
  • 温良石一块 - Means 'one piece of Wenliang stone,' referring to a medicinal stone.
  • 令置金盒中，揩背上 - Means 'ordered to place it in a golden box and apply it to his back,' describing a specific method of application.
  • 其夜疾良愈 - Means 'that night his illness greatly improved,' indicating a positive outcome.
  • 书山中石上 - Means 'written on a stone in the mountains,' referring to a physical inscription.
  • 帝命录二诗呈览 - Means 'the emperor ordered the poems to be copied and presented for his review,' indicating a formal act of appreciation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天池寺 - Refers to Tiantchi Temple, a real temple in China, which adds historical and geographical context.
  • 赤脚僧 - Refers to a barefoot monk, a common figure in Chinese monastic traditions.
  • 天眼尊者 - Refers to Tiantian Zunzhe, a divine or enlightened being, often associated with Buddhist or Taoist deities.
  • 颠仙 - Refers to Diandian Xian, a legendary or supernatural figure, possibly a Taoist immortal.
  • 庐山竹林寺 - Refers to Zhulin Temple on Mount Lu, a significant religious site in Chinese history.
  • 奉天门 - Refers to the Imperial Gate (Fengtian Gate), a significant location in the imperial palace, symbolizing imperial authority and blessing.
  • 金轮 - Refers to the golden wheel, a symbol of imperial power and universal rule.
  • 明君有道乾坤广 - Refers to a wise ruler with virtue, where '乾坤' symbolizes the universe or the world.

Proper Nouns

  • 天池寺 - Tiantchi Temple.
  • 赤脚僧 - Barefoot monk.
  • 天眼尊者 - Tiantian Zunzhe.
  • 颠仙 - Diandian Xian.
  • 庐山竹林寺 - Zhulin Temple on Mount Lu.
  • 奉天门 - Fengtian Gate.
  • 金轮 - Golden wheel.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 初見聖主廣天基 - Formal expression meaning 'first meeting with the sovereign to expand the heavens and earth,' likely referring to the emperor's reign or territory.
  • 逐片俱來箍一統 - Metaphorical phrase meaning 'all pieces come together to form a unified whole,' suggesting the consolidation of power or territory.
  • 浩大乾坤正此時 - Poetic expression meaning 'the vast universe is in its proper time,' indicating a moment of great significance.
  • 人君自此安邦定 - Means 'the ruler will establish peace and stability from this point,' emphasizing the emperor's role in governance.
  • 我王感得龍顏喜 - Means 'the king feels the emperor's pleased expression,' indicating the emperor's satisfaction.
  • 大興佛法當此時 - Means 'greatly promoting Buddhism at this time,' suggesting a period of religious flourishing.
  • 跣足慇懃事有秋 - Phrase meaning 'barefoot and diligent, there will be a harvest,' indicating hard work leading to success.
  • 空苦颠际孰为俦 - Poetic expression meaning 'in the midst of hardship and madness, who can be a companion?' suggesting isolation and struggle.
  • 愆销累世冤魂断 - Means 'sins are expiated, and the grievances of past generations are severed,' indicating the resolution of past wrongs.
  • 幻脱富时业海愁 - Metaphorical phrase meaning 'escaping the illusions of wealth and the troubles of worldly affairs,' suggesting a transcendence of material concerns.
  • 方广昔闻仙委迹 - Means 'in the past, I heard of the traces of immortals in the vast,' suggesting a connection to the divine or supernatural.
  • 天池今见佛来由 - Means 'now I see the reason for the Buddha's appearance in the Heavenly Pool,' indicating a spiritual revelation.
  • 神怜黔首增吾寿 - Means 'the gods pity the common people and increase my lifespan,' suggesting divine favor and longevity.
  • 丹饵来临久疾瘳 - Means 'the elixir of immortality arrives, and long-standing illnesses are cured,' indicating healing and rejuvenation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 周颠仙 - Refers to Zhou Dianxian, a legendary figure in Chinese folklore known for his eccentric behavior and spiritual wisdom.
  • 赤脚僧 - Refers to a monk who walks barefoot, symbolizing humility and asceticism.
  • 洪武二十六年癸酉九月 - Refers to the 26th year of the Hongwu era (1393 AD), during the reign of Emperor Hongwu of the Ming Dynasty.
  • 匡庐白鹿异仙台 - Refers to a sacred site in Mount Lu (Kunlun), associated with immortals and spiritual practices.
  • 莲花寺 - Refers to the Lotus Temple, a Buddhist temple.
  • 庐山天池寺 - Refers to the Heavenly Pool Temple on Mount Lu, a famous Buddhist monastery.

Proper Nouns

  • 周颠仙 - Zhou Dianxian, a legendary figure.
  • 赤脚僧 - Barefoot Monk, a monk who walks barefoot.
  • 洪武 - The era name of Emperor Hongwu of the Ming Dynasty.
  • 匡庐 - Mount Lu, a sacred mountain in China.
  • 白鹿异仙台 - A sacred site on Mount Lu.
  • 莲花寺 - The Lotus Temple, a Buddhist temple.
  • 庐山天池寺 - The Heavenly Pool Temple on Mount Lu.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有仙术 - Means 'possessed divine arts' or 'had magical abilities.'
  • 所言时事，悉征验 - Means 'the things he said about current events were all verified,' indicating his prophetic abilities.
  • 人多崇信之 - Means 'people greatly revered and believed in him.'
  • 忽留颂而逝 - Means 'he suddenly left behind a poem and passed away.'
  • 临窆，觉棺中层动有声 - Means 'when they were about to bury him, they heard sounds coming from inside the coffin.'
  • 发视之，乃复生 - Means 'they opened the coffin to see, and he came back to life.'
  • 事皆先见，叩之响应 - Means 'all his predictions came true, and when asked, he responded appropriately.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 张三丰 - A legendary figure in Chinese history, associated with Taoism and martial arts.
  • 元末 - The end of the Yuan Dynasty (1271-1368).
  • 宝鸡金台观 - Jintai Temple in Baoji, a place where Zhang Sanfeng lived.
  • 洪武二十四年 - The 24th year of the Hongwu era (1391), during the Ming Dynasty.
  • 永乐中 - During the Yongle era (1402-1424), also during the Ming Dynasty.
  • 天顺三年 - The third year of the Tian Shun era (1459), during the Ming Dynasty.
  • 通微显化真人 - A posthumous Taoist title, indicating his spiritual status.
  • 武当山 - Mount Wudang, a famous Taoist mountain in Hubei Province, associated with Zhang Sanfeng and the development of internal martial arts.

Proper Nouns

  • 张三丰 - A historical figure.
  • 杨軏山 - A local man.
  • 胡濙 - A government official.
  • 任一愚 - A Taoist priest.
  • 杨永吉 - A military commander.
  • 孙碧云 - A Taoist priest.
  • 通微显化真人 - A posthumous Taoist title.
  • 武当山 - Mount Wudang.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 竟失所在 - Mysteriously lost or disappeared without a trace.
  • 火煅服之愈 - Burned and consumed the 'middle sleeve,' then was cured. Metaphorical description of a miraculous cure.
  • 语意清旷 - Clear and lofty meaning, as if describing himself.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鹤鸣山 - A famous mountain in Sichuan, associated with Daoist hermits and immortals.
  • 甘州 - An ancient name for Gansu Province, a region in northwest China.
  • 天顺 - The era name of the Ming dynasty emperor Jingtai.
  • 成化 - The era name of the Ming dynasty emperor Chenghua.
  • 定西侯 - A title held by a high-ranking military officer during the Ming dynasty.
  • 琼花观 - A temple or garden in Yangzhou known for its famous peony flowers.
  • 三度城南杂剧 - A specific type of drama or play performed in the southern part of the city.

Proper Nouns

  • 鹤鸣山 - Hèmíng Mountains.
  • 甘州 - Gan Prefecture.
  • 天顺 - Tian Shun period.
  • 成化 - Chenghua period.
  • 定西侯 - Dingxi Hou.
  • 琼花观 - Qionghua Temple.
  • 三度城南杂剧 - 'Three Times Southern City' drama.

[10]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 书 - To write or record.
  • 事 - Story or event.
  • 乃 - Indicates that something is indeed or truly.
  • 旧传 - Old account or tradition.
  • 鄙拙 - Vulgar and clumsy.
  • 删润 - To revise and polish.
  • 邋遢 - Slovenly or untidy.
  • 灵济宫 - Lingji Palace, a Daoist temple.
  • 刻本 - Inscribed text.
  • 文皇御书 - The Emperor's decree.
  • 玄玄子 - A title.
  • 三丰先生 - A title for Sanfeng.
  • 举人 - Scholar.
  • 内徙 - To move inward or to relocate.
  • 酷慕 - Deeply enamored.
  • 不能知 - Cannot know.
  • 不复别出 - Did not emerge again.
  • 玉堂漫笔 - A historical or literary work that records various accounts and stories.
  • 宦游 - To travel as an official.
  • 续录 - Continued recording.
  • 于左 - To the left.
  • 但曰 - Only says.
  • 天师之后 - After the Celestial Master.
  • 与懿志不合 - Does not match the Yizhou Gazetteer.
  • 岂 - Is it.
  • 仲安 - A name, possibly referring to a descendant.
  • 即 - Indeed.
  • 苗裔 - Descendant, often used in historical and genealogical contexts.
  • 存疑 - To leave in doubt.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 三丰 - A legendary figure in Chinese Daoism, often associated with longevity and martial arts.
  • 懿州志 - The Gazetteer of Yizhou, a historical record of the Yizhou region.
  • 灵济宫 - A Daoist temple known for its historical and religious significance.
  • 文皇御书 - The Emperor's decree, a formal document issued by the emperor.
  • 玉堂漫笔 - A historical or literary work that records various accounts and stories.
  • 晋蜀荆湘 - Names of regions in ancient China, often associated with cultural and historical significance.
  • 天师 - The Celestial Master, a title given to the leader of the Celestial Master Daoist sect.
  • 苗裔 - Descendant, often used in historical and genealogical contexts.

Proper Nouns

  • 三丰 - A legendary figure in Chinese Daoism.
  • 懿州志 - The Gazetteer of Yizhou.
  • 灵济宫 - A Daoist temple.
  • 文皇御书 - The Emperor's decree.
  • 玉堂漫笔 - A historical or literary work.
  • 晋蜀荆湘 - Names of regions in ancient China.
  • 天师 - The Celestial Master.
  • 仲安 - A name, possibly referring to a descendant.

[11]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 訪邋遢張仙人 - Literal meaning 'visit the disheveled Immortal Zhang,' referring to a legendary figure known for his eccentricity and spiritual prowess.
  • 辭還山 - Literal meaning 'declined and returned to the mountains,' indicating a retreat from worldly affairs.
  • 寓居鳳翔寶雞縣之金台觀修煉 - Literal meaning 'dwelled in the Jintai Temple in Baoji County, Fengxiang, for cultivation.'
  • 常應蜀獻王之召 - Literal meaning 'frequently responded to the invitation of the Prince of Shu.'
  • 訪邋遢張仙人 - Literal meaning 'visit the disheveled Immortal Zhang,' referring to a legendary figure known for his eccentricity and spiritual prowess.
  • 名通，號玄玄子 - Literal meaning 'His given name was Tong, and his courtesy name was Xuanxuanzi.'
  • 天師之後 - Literal meaning 'descended from the Celestial Master,' referring to the head of the Taoist Celestial Masters sect.
  • 元時於鹿邑之太清宮學道 - Literal meaning 'during the Yuan Dynasty, he studied Daoism at the Taqing Palace in Luyi.'
  • 與朝用高祖毅相識 - Literal meaning 'he met Zhang Chaoyong's great-grandfather, Yi.'
  • 往來其家為親密 - Literal meaning 'they visited each other's homes and became close.'
  • 亦愛朝用之父叔廉 - Literal meaning 'he also loved Zhang Chaoyong's father, Shu Lian.'
  • 元末兵亂 - Literal meaning 'the turmoil at the end of the Yuan Dynasty.'
  • 叔廉避地寶雞 - Literal meaning 'Shu Lian fled to Baoji.'
  • 洪武中 - Literal meaning 'during the middle years of the Hongwu Emperor's reign.'
  • 西關李道士白雲先生 - Literal meaning 'a Taoist hermit named Baiyun, likely a friend or associate of Zhang Sanfeng.'
  • 朝用時方年十三 - Literal meaning 'Zhang Chaoyong was thirteen years old at the time.'
  • 童子其勉力讀書 - Literal meaning 'as a child, he should strive to study diligently.'
  • 後當官至三品 - Literal meaning 'later he will hold an official position of the third rank.'

  • 洪武壬辰 - Refers to the Renchen year of the Hongwu era (1392), the reign of the Hongwu Emperor (Zhu Yuanzhang).

  • 宗人府 - An administrative office in the Ming dynasty responsible for managing the royal family's affairs.
  • 建文君 - Refers to the Jianwen Emperor (Zhu Yunwen), who ruled from 1399 to 1402.
  • 燕藩 - Refers to the Yanzhou princely fief, held by Zhu Di (later the Yongle Emperor), who later rebelled against the Jianwen Emperor.
  • 靖難師 - Refers to the Jingnan Rebellion (1399–1402), a civil war initiated by Zhu Di against the Jianwen Emperor.
  • 文廟 - Refers to the Yongle Emperor (Zhu Di), who succeeded the Jianwen Emperor and ruled from 1402 to 1424.
  • 姚廣孝 - A prominent monk and advisor to the Yongle Emperor, known for his influence during the Jingnan Rebellion.
  • 私諡 - Refers to a private posthumous title given to a deceased person, often in contrast to official posthumous titles.
  • 忠貞 - A posthumous title meaning 'Loyal and Faithful,' often given to individuals who demonstrated exceptional loyalty and integrity.
  • 盧相 - Refers to Lu Xiang, a historical figure whose specific actions are not detailed in the text.
  • 卓忠貞 - Refers to Zhu Zhongzhen, a historical figure known for his loyalty and faithfulness.
  • 憫忠貞忠孝天性 - Refers to the concept of innate loyalty and filial piety, which were highly valued in Confucian ethics.
  • 公求生之謀 - Refers to the public's strategy for survival, often in the context of political turmoil or conflict.
  • 鬼神恍惚 - Refers to the mysterious and unpredictable nature of supernatural forces, often discussed in Confucian and Daoist thought.
  • 臆決 - Refers to making decisions based on personal conjecture or intuition, rather than concrete evidence.
  • 謹備錄 - Refers to the careful and meticulous recording of information, often used in historical and literary contexts.
  • 所聞如此 - Refers to the statement 'that is what I have heard,' indicating that the information is based on hearsay or second-hand accounts.

  • 迢迢出采石，郁郁见黄州 - Describes a journey through a dense and thick environment, possibly with a metaphorical meaning. '迢迢' means 'far and distant,' and '郁郁' suggests a dense or thick environment. '采石' and '黄州' are likely place names.
  • 蜀船三峡来，两岸猿声哀 - Describes a boat journey through the Three Gorges, with the mournful cries of apes on both sides of the river. '蜀船三峡来' means 'a boat from Shu (Sichuan) comes through the Three Gorges,' and '两岸猿声哀' describes the mournful cries of apes on both sides of the river.

  • 诸客大惊愕追留之，叫啸入林薄中，不知所在 - Describes the guests' reaction to the sudden disappearance of the four young men. '诸客大惊愕' means 'the guests were greatly surprised,' '追留之' means 'tried to stop them,' and '叫啸入林薄中，不知所在' describes their sudden disappearance into the forest.
  • 乃诣李监船问槛京师罪人袁氏，无所得，独前舟有一猴子在阱中 - Describes the search for the Yuan family and the discovery of a monkey in a trap. '乃诣李监船' means 'they went to Li's boat,' '问槛京师罪人袁氏' means 'to ask about the Yuan family, who were criminals in the capital,' '无所得' means 'nothing was found,' and '独前舟有一猴子在阱中' means 'only a monkey was found in a trap on the previous boat.'
  • 三人归舟，发其行李，惟橡栗三箧而已 - Describes the contents of the luggage of the three people. '三人归舟' means 'the three returned to their boat,' '发其行李' means 'opened their luggage,' and '惟橡栗三箧而已' means 'only three boxes of acorns and chestnuts were found.'
  • 共诣李详言其事，李监悯然，开阱纵之 - Describes the release of the monkey by Li. '共诣李详言其事' means 'they all went to Li to explain the matter,' '李监悯然' means 'Li was moved with pity,' and '开阱纵之' means 'he opened the trap and released the monkey.'

  • 岂论遗骨葬江鱼 - Line meaning 'Who cares if my remains are buried in the river,' with '遗骨' referring to one’s bones or remains.
  • 百年臣子悲何极 - Line meaning 'A hundred years of a minister’s sorrow knows no bounds,' with '百年臣子' referring to a long-serving official.

  • 至元得禮部度牒為僧 - '至元' is the era name of Kublai Khan, '得禮部度牒' means 'obtained a certificate from the Ministry of Rites,' and '為僧' means 'became a monk.'

  • 間接命於真定 - '間接命' is ambiguous; it could mean 'indirectly extended his life' or 'met someone who extended his life,' and '真定' is the name of a place, now part of Shijiazhuang.
  • 遇群盗夺其驴，刀伤其身面二三处 - '遇群盗' means 'met a group of bandits,' '夺其驴' means 'took his donkey,' and '刀伤其身面二三处' means 'cut him in two or three places on his body and face.'
  • 遂入滇南山中避乱 - '遂入滇南山中' means 'then entered the mountains of southern Yunnan,' and '避乱' means 'to avoid chaos.'
  • 泰中时往来荆襄陕洛间 - '泰中时' is ambiguous; it could mean 'during the Taizhong era' or 'in the middle of the Taizong era,' and '往来荆襄陕洛间' means 'wandered between Jing, Xiang, Shan, and Luo regions.'
  • 人尚未之知也 - '人尚未之知也' means 'people did not yet know about him.'
  • 成化末过江西 - '成化末' means 'at the end of the Chenghua era,' and '过江西' means 'passed through Jiangxi.'
  • 有宗室扣其术不答 - '有宗室' means 'a member of the royal family,' '扣其术' means 'asked about his technique,' and '不答' means 'did not answer.'

  • 令左右置棺中舁出，生焚之 - '令左右置棺中舁出' means 'ordered his attendants to place him in a coffin and carry him out,' and '生焚之' means 'burned him alive.'

  • 游南都成国公见所佩元朝羊皮度牒，始共骇异，知其年且二百余矣 - '游南都' means 'wandered in Nanjing,' '成国公' is the title of a noble, '见所佩元朝羊皮度牒' means 'saw the Yuan Dynasty sheepskin certificate he wore,' '始共骇异' means 'they were shocked and amazed,' and '知其年且二百余矣' means 'they realized he was over two hundred years old.'

  • 瑾闻遣使至，释之 - Jin heard that envoys had arrived and released him.
  • 遂以妖言惑众，缉送法司议死 - He was then accused of spreading false doctrines and misleading the people, and was arrested and sent to the judicial authorities for the death penalty.
  • 止令招年九十，免死押发原籍钳束 - He only ordered Yin to admit he was ninety years old, thus avoiding the death penalty and being sent back to his hometown under house arrest.
  • 一日，土民修殿发土，土中得铁鹤 - One day, local people were repairing a temple and found an iron crane in the soil.
  • 对众高揖而去 - He bowed to the crowd and then left.
  • 初在刑部问官叩其术合用妇人否 - When Yin was first questioned by the officials at the Criminal Department, they asked if his method involved women.
  • 自言每一接命必得奇祸 - He said that every time he received an order, he would suffer a strange misfortune.
  • 对问官惟请死期 - He only asked for the date of his execution.
  • 一食能尽胡饼数十，酒数斗 - He could eat dozens of flatbreads and drink several dou of wine in one meal.
  • 或数日不与亦未尝告饥 - Even if he was not given food for several days, he never complained of hunger.
  • 平居惟单衣袍隆冬不寒 - In his daily life, he wore only a single-layer robe, and he did not feel cold even in severe winter.
  • 及遣发日，忽向人求纩袄 - On the day he was sent away, he suddenly asked for a cotton coat.
  • 甫出门脱付解人 - As soon as he stepped out the door, he took off the coat and handed it to the escort.
  • 秦地苦寒，特求此赠耳 - The region of Qin is very cold, so he just asked for this as a gift.
  • 前所佩羊皮度牒，刘瑾收之 - The sheepskin document he had worn earlier was taken by Liu Jin.
  • 瑾死，用以殉葬 - After Liu Jin's death, it was used to accompany him in his burial.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 正德 - The era name of the Ming Emperor Zhengde (r. 1506–1521).
  • 东厂 - The Eastern Depot, a secret police organization during the Ming Dynasty.
  • 法司 - The judicial authorities or the Ministry of Justice.
  • 刑书 - The chief clerk of the Criminal Department.
  • 铁鹤馆 - The Iron Crane Pavilion, a place where Yin lived.
  • 羊皮度牒 - A sheepskin document, often used as a religious or official credential.
  • 殉葬 - The practice of burying objects or people with the deceased.

Proper Nouns

  • 瑾 - A person's name, likely referring to a historical figure.
  • 刘瑾 - Liu Jin, a powerful eunuch during the Ming Dynasty.
  • 赖义 - Lai Yi, a eunuch in charge of the Eastern Depot.
  • 尹 - Yin, the subject of the story.
  • 闵公珪 - Min Gong Gui, the chief clerk of the Criminal Department.
  • 铁鹤 - The Iron Crane, a magical artifact in the story.

  • 食能兼数人 - An expression indicating that one meal can sustain multiple people, suggesting extraordinary consumption.
  • 两手指掌，拳曲不舒 - Describes the physical state of the person, with fingers curled and unable to straighten.
  • 前星未耀 - A metaphorical expression referring to the period before the enthronement of a new emperor.
  • 太玄真人 - A title given to a Daoist sage, indicating high spiritual attainment.
  • 今上御极初 - Refers to the early reign of the current emperor.
